meta.title: aish — the AI-native shell
meta.description: aish is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers. v0.2 introduces a plugin system, with aish commit available as a plugin. Free software, MIT-licensed.

hero.badge: v0.2 shipped — plugin system is live

subhead.text: is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers. v0.2 introduces a subprocess plugin system, with
available as the first official plugin.

hero.installCaption: Linux & macOS · x86_64 / arm64 · or build from source with cargo.

features.heading: What aish is designed to be

items.title: Extensible Plugins
items.body: v0.2 introduces a subprocess plugin system. Install tools like aish commit via aish plugin install. Plugins run in isolated stdio loops with strict timeouts, bounded stderr, and integrity checks.

items.title: Provider-agnostic
items.body: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, and Kilo behind one config and model alias. Never locked to a single vendor — swap models with a flag.

items.title: Open & inspectable
items.body: MIT-licensed free software, shipped as a single static Rust binary. Read the specs, audit the design, send a patch — built in the open from day one.
